commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7bd6074e254cc99454dd3d4c165686dfefc77022)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a core `openclaw snapshot` CLI for SQLite snapshot create, verify, restore, and list flows, plus local snapshot artifacts, named database targets, memory-search path configuration, docs, and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +775, Tests +834, Docs +111. Total +1720 across 25 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the PR head adds `memorySearch.store.path` schema/runtime use while current main's doctor migration removes that key. No runtime test was run because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The protected maintainer-labeled draft needs a maintainer decision on whether the memory-search path config may be revived before any repair lane should proceed.

target.title: Add core SQLite snapshot command
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a core SQLite snapshot command for OpenClaw-owned state.

It includes:
- shared SQLite snapshot provider and local artifact repository
- `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list`
- named OpenClaw database targets: `--target global`, `--agent <id>`, and `--target memory-search --agent <id>`
- explicit `--db <path>` support for advanced/manual SQLite files
- config support for `agents.defaults.memorySearch.store.path`, including `{agentId}` substitution
- docs for which files are safe to sync and which live SQLite sidecars should be ignored
- docs that distinguish `openclaw snapshot` from `openclaw backup`

`openclaw snapshot` is the narrow per-database SQLite sync artifact. `openclaw backup` remains the broader `.tar.gz` recovery archive for state, config, credentials, sessions, and optional workspaces.

## Why

The goal from #94675 is not to make hosts copy live SQLite files more carefully. The goal is for OpenClaw to produce a SQLite-safe sync artifact.

This PR establishes that boundary:
- live `.sqlite`, `-wal`, and `-shm` files remain hot local runtime state
- `openclaw snapshot create` materializes a portable artifact directory containing `manifest.json` and `database.sqlite`
- hosts, containers, object-store sync jobs, and backup systems should sync the completed snapshot directory, not the live SQLite file family
- restore hydrates a fresh local SQLite file from the verified artifact before OpenClaw opens it

The Lobster-shaped case is now first-class: hosted containers can keep memory-search SQLite on local/ephemeral disk, for example `/tmp/memory/{agentId}.sqlite`, and ask core for `openclaw snapshot create --target memory-search --agent main` instead of hardcoding that path in the host.

## Stack

This PR combines the previous core implementation slices:
- former #94694: core snapshot provider proof
- former #94717: core `openclaw snapshot` CLI
- former #94799: named OpenClaw database targets
- this PR: safe-sync artifact, restore proof/docs, and configured memory-search snapshot target

Follow-up validation PR: #94967
RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20
Related: #94675, #94646

## Scope

This does not add WAL bundle deltas, object storage upload, scheduling, leases, failover automation, restore-on-boot behavior, or production orchestration. Those remain future phases. This PR ships the local core command and artifact contract.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
OpenClaw can now convert OpenClaw-owned SQLite state into a syncable artifact directory, including a Lobster-shaped memory-search SQLite DB configured outside `.openclaw`, verify it, copy it independently from the live SQLite files, and restore it into a verified fresh SQLite database.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, Node v22.22.3, `pnpm build` packaged output, `node openclaw.mjs` launcher commands, temporary local SQLite databases created with Node `node:sqlite`, local snapshot repositories under `C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof` and `.artifacts/snapshot-proof/pr94805`, plus source Vitest harnesses with WAL-mode SQLite databases, named OpenClaw database targets, and config schema validation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `pnpm build`
2. Create a temporary Lobster-shaped config with `agents.defaults.memorySearch.store.path = C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof\tmp-memory\{agentId}.sqlite`.
3. Create `C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof\tmp-memory\main.sqlite` with `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`, `PRAGMA user_version = 271`, and row `lobster-memory-search`.
4. `node scripts/run-with-env.mjs OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof\openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof\state -- node openclaw.mjs snapshot create --target memory-search --agent main --repository C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof\snapshots --json`
5. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof\snapshots\2026-06-19T18-01-49-181Z-main.sqlite-a8ddc96a-3f4a-4304-b232-cbf193d2a6fb --json`
6. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof\snapshots\2026-06-19T18-01-49-181Z-main.sqlite-a8ddc96a-3f4a-4304-b232-cbf193d2a6fb --target C:\tmp\openclaw-snapshot-lobster-proof\restore\memory-search.sqlite --json`
7. Read the restored DB with Node `node:sqlite` and query `SELECT value FROM entries ORDER BY value` plus `PRAGMA user_version`.
8. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/agents/memory-search.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
9. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/snapshot.ts src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/agents/memory-search.ts src/agents/memory-search.test.ts src/config/types.tools.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts docs/cli/snapshot.md`
10. `git diff --check`
11. `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
12. `pnpm docs:check-links`

Evidence after fix:
Packaged CLI output from memory-search create returned `ok: true`, `database.id: "agent:main:memory-search"`, `database.kind: "agent-memory-search"`, `database.basename: "main.sqlite"`, `database.userVersion: 271`, artifact path `database.sqlite`, artifact size `8192`, and a `sha256:<64 hex chars>` artifact hash.

Packaged CLI output from verify and restore returned `ok: true` and `integrityCheck: ["ok"]` for the same snapshot path.

Readback from the restored memory-search SQLite DB returned:

```json
{
  "rows": [
    { "value": "lobster-memory-search" }
  ],
  "userVersion": { "user_version": 271 }
}
```

The created memory-search snapshot directory contained exactly:

```json
[
  { "Name": "database.sqlite", "Length": 8192 },
  { "Name": "manifest.json", "Length": 472 }
]
```

Supporting validation passed:
- `pnpm build`
- Vitest passed 4 shards: `src/agents/memory-search.test.ts` (36 tests), `src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts` (35 tests), `src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts` (8 tests), and `src/commands/snapshot.test.ts` (7 tests)
- `oxfmt --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`

Observed result after fix:
The packaged CLI created a verified snapshot artifact from the configured memory-search SQLite database without passing `--db` or exposing the `/tmp/memory/{agentId}.sqlite` path to the host command. The artifact restored to a fresh SQLite file, preserved `PRAGMA user_version = 271`, and contained the expected `lobster-memory-search` row. This proves the host can sync `manifest.json` plus `database.sqlite` while OpenClaw core owns the database path resolution.

The source tests additionally cover WAL-mode SQLite snapshots, global named targets, per-agent named targets, configured memory-search paths, config schema acceptance for `memorySearch.store.path`, listing, verification, restore, stale sidecar cleanup, and explicit `--db` compatibility.

What was not tested:
No live gateway runtime, Azure Blob upload, Lobster adapter integration, scheduled backup loop, restore-on-boot path, lease/failover path, cross-platform stress, or WAL bundle/delta behavior was tested in this PR. Stress validation is tracked separately in #94967.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
